title: Gap Closure, Deliberate Practice, and Honest Positioning · Career Claim Defense
summary: A hiring panel compares near-miss decisions for gap closure, deliberate practice, and honest positioning and accepts only role-relevant, truthful, accessible, privacy-preserving, and independently defensible evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for time-boxed role-readiness experiment plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Prioritize gaps by role frequency, task centrality, transfer distance, evidence weakness, learning cost, and application timing.
Design the smallest authentic task that can reveal or improve one missing behavior under a changed case.
Precommit a time, evidence, and opportunity-cost stop rule for readiness work.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Gap Priority · changed case · Quiz Gap Priority
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Three genuine evidence gaps trigger a six-month general course plan even though only one may block the candidate’s target work. The quiz variation 6 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Prioritize gaps by role frequency, task centrality, transfer distance, evidence weakness, learning cost, and application timing.

Which action demonstrates Gap Priority without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: Gap Priority · changed case · Quiz Gap Priority.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for prioritize gaps by role frequency, task centrality, transfer distance, evidence weakness, learning cost, and application timing. For Gap Priority · changed case · Quiz Gap Priority, keep Gap Priority connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: Three genuine evidence gaps trigger a six-month general course plan even though only one may block the candidate’s target work. The quiz variation 6 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Prioritize gaps by role frequency, task centrality, transfer distance, evidence weakness, learning cost, and application timing.

content.title: Evidence inspection
content.text: Use the supplied trace to distinguish an observed Gap Priority result from an unsupported claim.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: CareerOneStop Job Search — Controls the connected search flow across planning, employer research, networking, applications, portfolios, interviews, background checks, and negotiation.
